description: UE5 camera: UCameraComponent, USpringArmComponent, PlayerCameraManager, camera blending, view targets, and camera shake

entries.title: Camera & Spring Arm Setup
entries.description: UCameraComponent defines what the player sees. USpringArmComponent creates a boom arm that handles collision avoidance and lag. Together they form the standard third-person camera.
entries.whatItIs: UCameraComponent renders the scene from its viewpoint to the player's screen. USpringArmComponent is a boom arm that positions the camera at a set distance behind the character, with built-in collision detection (pulls the camera forward when it would clip through walls) and optional rotation/position lag for smooth following.
whenToUse: Any third-person game — SpringArm + Camera on the character is the standard setup
First-person games — Camera attached directly to the character mesh head socket or a SceneComponent at eye height
Top-down or isometric games — Camera at a fixed angle with longer SpringArm and disabled collision
Side-scrollers — Camera with a SpringArm at a fixed Y offset
whenNotToUse: Fixed security camera views — use a CameraActor placed in the level and SetViewTarget
Cinematic cameras during cutscenes — use ACineCameraActor with Sequencer
When you need complex camera behaviors (orbit, rail, crane) — use the Camera Rig actors or custom PlayerCameraManager
commonMistakes: Attaching the Camera to the Mesh instead of the SpringArm — the camera jitters with every animation frame
Not enabling bUsePawnControlRotation on the SpringArm — the camera doesn't follow mouse/stick look
Setting bInheritPitch/Yaw/Roll wrong on the SpringArm — the camera tilts unexpectedly or doesn't rotate
Not using bDoCollisionTest on the SpringArm — the camera clips through walls in third-person
Setting CameraLagSpeed too low — the camera feels sluggish and disconnected from the character
keyPoints: USpringArmComponent: TargetArmLength (distance), bDoCollisionTest (wall avoidance), bUsePawnControlRotation (follow mouse)
Camera Lag: bEnableCameraLag, CameraLagSpeed — smooth follow instead of rigid attachment
Rotation Lag: bEnableCameraRotationLag, CameraRotationLagSpeed — smooth turn
UCameraComponent: FieldOfView, AspectRatio, PostProcessSettings — what the player sees
SetViewTargetWithBlend(): smoothly transition between cameras over time
PlayerCameraManager: handles camera shakes, fades, and view target management per PlayerController
Camera shake: UMatineeCameraShake or UCameraShakeBase — trigger with PlayerCameraManager->StartCameraShake()
